source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: Design the primary structure of a 3U CubeSat for a student flight-grade program as a multi-part bolted assembly, launching via a standard P-POD / ISIPOD-compliant deployer. The assembly consists of four long deployer rail frames (hard-anodized on exposed surface), six solar panel side plates (four body panels + a top optical plate + a bottom deployer-interface plate), internal bolted ribs / stack mounting ribs that carry payload stack shear, and the fastener / hardware set. Envelope must comply with Cal Poly CDS Rev 14.1: 100 mm x 100 mm x 340.5 mm maximum, with four deployer rails of 8.5 mm width on each corner, rail surface area minimum 75% hard-anodized aluminum, center of mass within +/- 2 cm of geometric center on each axis. Total wet mass must not exceed 4.0 kg. Material: primary chassis bus machined from Aluminum 7075-T6 (Young's modulus 71.7 GPa, Poisson 0.33, yield 503 MPa, ultimate 572 MPa, density 2810 kg/m^3). Load cases (flight-qualification): (LC1) quasi-static launch: 14 g simultaneous along each of the three body axes applied as inertia on wet mass; (LC2) random vibration: Cal Poly CDS 14.1 Grms qualification profile (frequency range 20-2000 Hz, ASD table from CDS), response must show first mode >= 100 Hz and stress margins at the rails; (LC3) sine vibration: 10-100 Hz, 2 g base sweep; (LC4) thermal gradient: -40 C to +85 C uniform swing representing orbit eclipse/insolation cycle, with a 30 C radial gradient from sun-side to shade-side panel. Pass/fail per NASA-STD-5001B metallic protoflight: yield FoS >= 1.25 and ultimate FoS >= 1.4 on the LC1 quasi-static load, i.e., max von Mises <= 503 / 1.25 = 402 MPa on yield gate and <= 572 / 1.4 = 409 MPa on ultimate. First natural frequency >= 100 Hz. Random-vib 3-sigma response stress <= 402 MPa. Thermal deformation of optical payload mounting plane <= 50 microns flatness across the full temperature range. Verification is performed on the assembled bolted model with representative fastener stiffness at every panel/rail interface; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Deliverables: STEP with all bodies, modal analysis (first 10 modes), quasi-static FEA, random-vib analysis with PSD response, thermal analysis with displacement contour, and structural mass + CM report.
short_prompt:
Design the primary structure of a 3U CubeSat for a student flight-grade program as a multi-part bolted assembly launching via a P-POD / ISIPOD-compliant deployer.

Envelope complies with Cal Poly CDS Rev 14.1: 100 mm x 100 mm x 340.5 mm maximum, four deployer rails of 8.5 mm width on each corner, >= 75% hard-anodized rail surface, CM within +/- 2 cm of geometric center. Total wet mass <= 4.0 kg. Chassis material Aluminum 7075-T6 (E 71.7 GPa, Poisson 0.33, yield 503 MPa, ultimate 572 MPa, density 2810 kg/m^3). Qualification per NASA-STD-5001B protoflight (yield FoS 1.25, ultimate FoS 1.4).

Load cases.
- LC1 quasi-static launch: 14 g simultaneous along each of the three body axes on wet mass.
- LC2 random vibration per CDS 14.1 Grms qualification profile, 20-2000 Hz ASD.
- LC3 sine vibration: 10-100 Hz, 2 g base sweep.
- LC4 thermal: -40 C to +85 C uniform swing with a 30 C radial gradient sun- to shade-side.

First mode >= 100 Hz; optical mounting flatness <= 50 microns across range.

prompt.title: Student 3U CubeSat primary structure (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: 3U CubeSat bus built as a bolted assembly of four corner rails, six panels (four sides + top optical + bottom deployer), internal payload-stack ribs, and fasteners; compliant with CDS Rev 14.1 and NASA-STD-5001B.

parts.name: corner_rail
parts.role: one of four deployer rails at the bus corners; carries P-POD rail contact load and guides ejection

parts.name: side_panel
parts.role: one of four body panels forming the ±X and ±Y bus faces, carrying solar cells and payload mount bosses

parts.name: top_optical_plate
parts.role: top-facing panel carrying the optical payload aperture and mounting interface

parts.name: bottom_deployer_plate
parts.role: bottom-facing plate forming the deployer interface with pusher contact zone

parts.name: internal_stack_rib
parts.role: internal bolted rib carrying payload PCB stack shear and tying side panels together

parts.name: fastener_set
parts.role: M3 stainless bolts + torque pads tying rails, panels, ribs into a rigid bolted box

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: +Z along long axis = deployer rail axis; origin at geometric center

load_cases.name: quasi_static_14g
load_cases.application: simultaneous on all three body axes

boundary_conditions.interfaces: panels bolted to rails along full length at M3 pitch; internal ribs bolted to panels; fastener stiffness representative of M3 torque-controlled joint
boundary_conditions.support: deployer rails constrained in P-POD idealization (frictional along rail, free in push direction)

prompt.objective: minimize wet mass while meeting all pass/fail criteria

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

interfaces.load_path: panel shear into rail along full length
top optical plate into rail ends
bottom deployer plate into rail ends
panel tie into internal payload-stack rib
fastener clamp force establishing bus rigidity

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: CDS Rev 14.1 CubeSat buses are bolted boxes of corner rails, side panels, top + bottom end plates, and internal ribs; flight hardware is never fabricated as a single monolithic piece.
notes.speculation_caveats: Rail/panel/rib counts, fastener types, and interface stiffness are speculative; materials and thresholds inherit the original sample verbatim.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
